mechanism_of_action: In Pseudomonas aeruginosa, colistin sulfate caused nuclear material to become non-demonstrable, cytoplasm to lose its granularity and become homogeneous, and cytoplasmic membrane deterioration. The nuclear material was described as solubilized. In Bacillus megaterium, nuclear areas were obscured by cytoplasmic material engulfment, and cytoplasm lost its granularity. Proposed mechanisms for B. megaterium include antibiotic penetration reacting with cytoplasm to obliterate nuclear areas, or reaction with the cell wall (apparent thickening) rendering it impermeable to metabolites, leading to upset cell metabolism and replacement of deoxyribonucleic acid by ribonucleic acid.
